questions: Should the timeline be fully interactive on phone, or is it acceptable to make phone-timeline read-only (tap to select, scrub to seek) and require iPad/desktop for drag/trim editing? This significantly changes Phase 2-3 scope.

Is there a target for minimum clip width on touch (e.g., clips shorter than 40px cannot be directly tapped/trimmed and need the inspector)? Very short clips are inherently hard to interact with on touch.
Should pinch-to-zoom anchor to the midpoint between fingers (standard map behavior) or to the playhead position? Midpoint is more intuitive but playhead-anchored keeps the edit point visible.
Are there plans for Apple Pencil-specific features (e.g., pencil draws selection rectangles, pencil pressure controls trim speed)? Or should pencil just behave like a precise mouse?
For multi-select on touch, is the '+' affordance toggle approach acceptable, or would you prefer a dedicated 'multi-select mode' toggle button in the toolbar that makes all taps additive?

success_criteria.criterion: Horizontal swipe on the timeline scrolls (does not accidentally move clips) on touch devices
Tap on a clip selects it on touch devices
Long-press on a selected clip initiates drag on touch devices
Pinch-to-zoom changes timeline scale on touch devices
Clip resize handles are at least 20px wide touch targets on coarse pointer devices
All existing mouse/trackpad interactions work unchanged when pointerType is mouse
All existing automated tests pass without modification (except tests explicitly updated for touch behavior)
Long-press on a clip opens context menu when clip is not moved
Phone inspector drawer shows precision trim controls (frame-level steppers) for selected clips
Apple Pencil uses desktop-precision thresholds (4px drag, 8px handles)
Selected clips have ARIA labels describing clip name, track, start time, and duration
Keyboard arrow keys move selection between clips when a clip is focused
Drag activation shows visual lift feedback (scale + shadow) on touch
iPad Split View and Stage Manager layout transitions work correctly
VoiceOver can navigate and announce clip selection, drag, and resize results
Haptic feedback fires on long-press activation on supported devices
Manual smoke test: full edit workflow (select, move, trim, zoom, undo) on iPhone Safari
Manual smoke test: full edit workflow on iPad Safari with keyboard and without

assumptions: Phone timeline will be fully interactive (not read-only) — the plan includes precision controls in the inspector drawer to compensate for small screen size.
Apple Pencil treated as a fine pointer (mouse-equivalent precision) with no pressure-specific features.
Pinch-to-zoom anchors to the midpoint between the two fingers, consistent with iOS conventions.
The existing pointer event model (pointerdown/move/up/cancel) is sufficient — no need for the older TouchEvent API since all target browsers support pointer events.
Long-press threshold of 300ms matches iOS system conventions and will feel natural. No need for a user-configurable threshold.
The existing `useIsTouchDevice()` and `useIsMobile()` hooks correctly detect touch capability — no new detection logic needed.
Haptic feedback via navigator.vibrate() is progressive enhancement only — the interaction must work without it.
No new external dependencies needed — everything can be built with pointer events, CSS touch-action, and existing hooks.
